title: Distictella mansoana
intro: Distictella mansoana, also known as Manso's distictella, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for the treatment of various health issues such as fever, pain, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Distictella mansoana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory tract infections.
taxonomy: Distictella mansoana is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family includes around 600 genera and 13,000 species, many of which are economically important. The genus Distictella itself is part of the subfamily Ixoroideae, which is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their showy flowers and woody stems. Members of the subfamily Ixoroideae are primarily found in tropical regions and are often used as ornamental plants. The classification of Distictella mansoana reflects its evolutionary relationships and ecological adaptations within its native habitat.
